entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of production (meaning, types, factors and importance)
teaching_learning_activities: Observation activity: Provide students with tangible things such as bottles, shoes, books, furniture items, and clothes then ask them to explain how each one is made. Relate their responses to the meaning of production

Field trip: Assign students in manageable groups to pay a visit to any nearby production centre within the community to observe and learn about the production process, specifically on the: (i) types of production and the product produced; (ii) factors of productions used; and (iii) importance of the production to the community
Let students in groups share their findings with the rest of the class through presentations. Relate their findings to types of production, factors of production and importance of production
entries.assessment: The concept of production is clearly explained
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of production (meaning, types, factors and importance)
teaching_learning_activities: Observation activity: Provide students with tangible things such as bottles, shoes, books, furniture items, and clothes then ask them to explain how each one is made. Relate their responses to the meaning of production

Field trip: Assign students in manageable groups to pay a visit to any nearby production centre within the community to observe and learn about the production process, specifically on the: (i) types of production and the product produced; (ii) factors of productions used; and (iii) importance of the production to the community
Let students in groups share their findings with the rest of the class through presentations. Relate their findings to types of production, factors of production and importance of production
entries.assessment: The concept of production is clearly explained
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of production (meaning, types, factors and importance)
teaching_learning_activities: Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to think about and choose three products that they can produce in their business club and analyse how to produce the products based on the factors of production available in the school community. Then each group should choose one product that can be best produced with the factors of production available in their community
Let groups share their ideas with the rest of the class through presentations
Project activity: Task students in their business club to start production of products selected in the Skills lab. Monitor the progress of the students in this project from time to time
Students in groups write a simple report on the production of products
entries.assessment: The concept of production is clearly explained
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of production (meaning, types, factors and importance)
teaching_learning_activities: Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to think about and choose three products that they can produce in their business club and analyse how to produce the products based on the factors of production available in the school community. Then each group should choose one product that can be best produced with the factors of production available in their community
Let groups share their ideas with the rest of the class through presentations
Project activity: Task students in their business club to start production of products selected in the Skills lab. Monitor the progress of the students in this project from time to time
Students in groups write a simple report on the production of products
entries.assessment: The concept of production is clearly explained
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe sources of capital for small businesses (loans, savings, deferred payments, funds from family and friends)
teaching_learning_activities: Think-ink-pair-share: Ask students to think and write how can they get capital when they want to start a small business
Guide students to pair up to discuss their ideas. Thereafter, some of the pairs share their ideas with the whole class
Relate their findings with the meaning of various sources of capital for small businesses (loans, savings, deferred payments, funds from family, friends, grants and calculation of interest)

Field trip: Organise students in manageable groups to visit businesspersons from their community and request the information about the source of their capital when they started their businesses and the pros and cons of each source of capital in facilitating business startup
entries.assessment: The sources of capital for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe sources of capital for small businesses (loans, savings, deferred payments, funds from family and friends)
teaching_learning_activities: Let groups share their findings with the rest of the class. Relate their findings with different sources of capital and importance of capital in facilitating business startup
Guest speaker: Invite a guest speaker to share how they got their capital, what sources of capital are available, and importance of capital
Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups, each group should select source(s) of capital (to fund their business club project) that they can access based on their context

Guide groups to share their ideas with the rest of the class through presentations. Then, summarise the main points of the lesson, answer any questions, and give a practical assignment to be performed in the business club
Project activity: Task students to work in small groups to make plans for how they will get the capital suggested in the Skills lab
entries.assessment: The sources of capital for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (c) Describe the role of microfinancing and cooperatives in facilitating business formation and operations
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Organise students in manageable groups and assign each group a task of identifying various financial institutions that provide small loans (microcredits) to small business owners. Relate their ideas with the meaning and importance of microfinances
Scenario: Prepare different scenarios that portray the role of microfinances and cooperatives in facilitating business formation and operations
Guide students to summarise the lessons learned from the scenario
Relate the scenario to the meaning, importance and role of microfinances and cooperatives in facilitating business formation and operations
Field trip: Task students in manageable groups to visit nearby microfinances and cooperatives to learn how they facilitate small business formation and operations
Let groups share their findings with the rest of the class through presentations
entries.assessment: The role of microfinancing and cooperatives in facilitating business formation and operations is clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (c) Describe the role of microfinancing and cooperatives in facilitating business formation and operations
teaching_learning_activities: Relate their findings with the role of microfinances and cooperatives in facilitating business formation and operations

Project activity: Let the students share the feedback of their field trip to other members in the club and design a way of financing their club activities through microfinances and cooperatives available in their community
entries.assessment: The role of microfinancing and cooperatives in facilitating business formation and operations is clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of operating a small-scale business
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe the basic aspects of management for small businesses (meaning, financial and other records keeping, calculation of profit and loss, budgeting and control and administration)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups to identify the roles of a head of school, head of a family, owner of a business, business clubs’ leader, and the head prefect. Relate their ideas to the meaning and functions of management

Think-ink-pair-share: Ask students to think and make a list of the sources of income for an individual or a family for a given period and let them show how the income is spent in relation to the needs and wants of an individual or family
Let students share their ideas with the rest of the class
Guide the students to assess the difference between income and expenses and relate their ideas to the meaning and significance of a budget
Project activity: Task students in manageable groups to prepare a monthly cash budget based on their business club activities, using a budget template provided to them
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of management for small businesses are described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of operating a small-scale business
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe the basic aspects of warehousing and inventorying for small businesses (meaning and functions)
teaching_learning_activities: Group activity: Organise students in manageable groups to analyse different goods sold in a business (manufacturing or trading business)
Let them discuss how these items are stored, and the likely documents used to manage them
Guide the groups to present their findings in the class and relate their ideas with the meaning, types and functions of warehousing and inventorying
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of warehousing and inventorying for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of operating a small-scale business
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe the basic aspects of warehousing and inventorying for small businesses (meaning and functions)
teaching_learning_activities: Guest speaker: Invite a school procurement and supply officer or store keeper or expert from outside to share with students about warehousing and essential documents for inventory management

Let students in manageable groups to explain: (i) Different documents used in businesses to manage inventory and their importance; (ii) Ways they will use the documents to manage stock for the business club; and (iii) The methods used for management of inventory
Guide each group to share the findings
Project activity: Task each group to discuss and prepare inventory documents that will be used for their business club’s activities
Guide each group to present their documents to the club leadership and identify documents to be used by the business club
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of warehousing and inventorying for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Apply business knowledge in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Apply business theories into practice
entries.learning_activity: (a) Identify business opportunities available in the local environment
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Organise students into manageable groups and assign each group to work on a single economic sector, requiring them to identify business ideas that can be implemented in their local area based on their context

Gallery walk: Invite each group to record their ideas on a loose sheet such as flipchart, manilla card, A4 and display it on the wall. Ask each group to walk around and observe what the other group has written and to contribute any missing business ideas. Let them rotate to the next loose sheet until the cycle is complete
Ask students to agree on a few ideas that are most applicable in their environment

Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss and come up with any business opportunity(ies) based on their surroundings, then let the groups, through a presentation, share their identified opportunity(ies)
entries.assessment: The business opportunities available in the local environment are identified
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Apply business knowledge in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Apply business theories into practice
entries.learning_activity: (a) Identify business opportunities available in the local environment
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Organise students into manageable groups and assign each group to work on a single economic sector, requiring them to identify business ideas that can be implemented in their local area based on their context

Gallery walk: Invite each group to record their ideas on a loose sheet such as flipchart, manilla card, A4 and display it on the wall. Ask each group to walk around and observe what the other group has written and to contribute any missing business ideas. Let them rotate to the next loose sheet until the cycle is complete
Ask students to agree on a few ideas that are most applicable in their environment

Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss and come up with any business opportunity(ies) based on their surroundings, then let the groups, through a presentation, share their identified opportunity(ies)
entries.assessment: The business opportunities available in the local environment are identified
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Apply business knowledge in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Apply business theories into practice
entries.learning_activity: (a) Identify business opportunities available in the local environment
teaching_learning_activities: Skills lab: Guide students in groups to prepare a business pitch guide (key items to cover in business pitch guide include: Business ideas and goals, Problem identified, Solution, Target Market, Marketing strategy, Industry analysis, Management team, Competition, current status, amount of money requested and the projected use of funds), and present it
Provide feedback for improvement using a pitch rubric
Project activity: Task students in groups to polish the business pitch guide, role play (rehearse) it, and pitch their business ideas in the business club or to a panel of investors
entries.assessment: The business opportunities available in the local environment are identified
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form Two: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education
